groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Topic, goal, audience, message, language, mode, length, and CTA.

groups.title: Message & Evidence
groups.description: Supporting points, offer terms, proof, disclosures, and restrictions.

groups.title: Brand & Style
groups.description: Voice, tone, emoji use, and hashtag strategy.

groups.title: Visual & Output
groups.description: Visual format, asset notes, alt text, and variants.

variables.label: Post topic
variables.hint: Enter the product, idea, announcement, story, event, or subject.

help.why: The model cannot safely determine the main subject without user input.
help.example: Honest Early Access product update for content teams
help.avoid: Several unrelated topics in one feed post.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.hint: Choose the single most important result the post should support.

help.why: The goal determines the post angle, structure, emphasis, and CTA.
help.example: Educate
help.avoid: Stacking sell + follow + share as equal primary goals.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Who should this post speak to?
variables.hint: Describe the intended reader, customer, follower, or community.

help.why: Audience context materially changes vocabulary, examples, benefits, and tone.
help.example: Who should this post speak to?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Core message
variables.placeholder: What should people understand or remember?
variables.hint: State the main takeaway rather than several competing messages.

help.why: It establishes the central idea around which the caption must be built.
help.example: One clear takeaway the reader should remember
help.avoid: Competing messages or unstated claims.

variables.label: Essential details
variables.placeholder: Add important names, dates, features, terms, or context.
variables.hint: Include details that must appear accurately in the post.

help.why: It prevents the model from guessing important factual information.
help.example: Add important names, dates, features, terms, or context.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Output language
variables.hint: Enter the language in which the complete post package should be written.

help.why: The publishing language cannot always be inferred from the topic or interface.
help.example: English
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Content mode
variables.hint: Choose a mode or let the model infer it from the brief.

help.why: Different Instagram post types require meaningfully different structures and voices.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Caption length
variables.hint: Platform appropriate chooses the shortest length that communicates the message well.

help.why: Desired depth cannot always be inferred from the topic alone.
help.example: Platform appropriate
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Desired action
variables.hint: Choose one reader action or let the model select a suitable CTA.

help.why: It controls the conversion action without requiring the user to write CTA copy.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Supporting points
variables.placeholder: Add benefits, examples, steps, objections, or secondary points.
variables.hint: Include only points that strengthen the main message.

help.why: It allows richer posts without overloading the main Core message field.
help.example: Add benefits, examples, steps, objections, or secondary points.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Offer details
variables.placeholder: Describe the offer, price, availability, dates, and terms.
variables.hint: Use exact confirmed information and leave unknown details out.

help.why: Promotional posts require exact terms that the model must not invent.
help.example: Confirmed price, dates, and terms only
help.avoid: Invented discounts, deadlines, or scarcity.

variables.label: Approved facts and proof
variables.placeholder: Add verified figures, results, quotes, credentials, or evidence.
variables.hint: Only include information approved for public use.

help.why: It enables evidence-based claims while preventing fabricated proof.
help.example: Add verified figures, results, quotes, credentials, or evidence.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Required disclosures
variables.placeholder: Add sponsorship, affiliate, gifted-product, or other disclosures.
variables.hint: Enter wording that must be visible in or near the caption.

help.why: Commercial relationships and regulated contexts may require explicit disclosure.
help.example: Add sponsorship, affiliate, gifted-product, or other disclosures.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add restrictions, required wording, exclusions, or other instructions.
variables.hint: Use this for anything important not covered by the other fields.

help.why: It provides one controlled location for exceptional requirements without duplicating fields.
help.example: Add restrictions, required wording, exclusions, or other instructions.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Describe how the account normally sounds.
variables.hint: Add a short voice description or examples of preferred phrasing.

help.why: A distinctive existing voice cannot be reliably inferred from the topic.
help.example: Describe how the account normally sounds.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Tone
variables.hint: Choose the closest overall tone; the model will keep it natural.

help.why: Tone materially changes sentence rhythm, vocabulary, and emotional intensity.
help.example: Clear and natural
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Emoji use
variables.hint: Controls frequency without requiring the user to choose individual emojis.

help.why: Emoji preferences vary significantly between accounts and audiences.
help.example: Minimal
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Hashtag strategy
variables.hint: Controls whether and how hashtags are included.

help.why: Hashtag use is an account-level preference that cannot always be inferred.
help.example: Niche mix
help.avoid: Unrelated trending tags or stuffing.

variables.label: Provided hashtags
variables.hint: Only these hashtags will be considered when Provided only is selected.

help.why: It supports controlled campaigns and approved brand hashtags without inventing alternatives.
help.example: #BrandTag #CampaignTag #TopicTag
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Visual format
variables.hint: Choose the available asset type or let the model recommend one.

help.why: Visual recommendations should match the asset the user can realistically publish.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Visual details or asset
variables.placeholder: Describe the image, asset, setting, subject, or visual restrictions.
variables.hint: Explain what is available or what the visual should communicate.

help.why: The model needs asset-specific context to avoid suggesting an unrelated visual.
help.example: Describe the image, asset, setting, subject, or visual restrictions.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Alt text
variables.hint: Auto includes alt text when the visual adds meaningful information.

help.why: It gives users control over accessibility output without forcing an extra field.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Number of variants
variables.hint: Generate one to three complete versions with different angles.

help.why: Some users need alternatives, but generating many versions by default would add noise.
help.avoid: More than three variants — keep noise low.
